system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    ( 0  20  0)       // 3
    ( 0  0  1)        // 4
    (20  0  1)        // 5
    (20  20  1)       // 6
    ( 0  20  1)       // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Ekp)    Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs

    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 36.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This has persisted across 9 attempts. The `species` keyword is not being found in `physicalProperties`. Looking at the current file content provided very carefully, I notice something critical:

The `physicalProperties` file currently has:

```
multiComponentMixture
{
    specie        specie;
    multiComponentAssemblies
    {
        assem1
        {
            reactions   ("CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O");
            specie      ("CH4" "O2" "CO2" "H2O");
        }
    }
}
```

This block is **missing** the `species` sub-dictionary. And the standalone `species` dictionary at the top level uses flat format (`CH4 16.043;`) instead of sub-dict format (`{ value 16.043; }`).

BUT — the key insight from all 9 failed attempts is that putting `species` inside `multiComponentMixture` didn't work. Now I realize the real issue: **the standalone `species` dictionary at the top level is commented out or malformed**.

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file content provided, there is NO standalone `species` dictionary visible anywhere in the file. The only `species` mention is inside `multiComponentAssemblies` as part of the `specie` list, which is not sufficient.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file is missing a top-level `species` dictionary entirely. Without it, the `multiComponentMixture` cannot find the species information it needs. The previous 9 attempts tried various combinations of moving `species` around, but the current file simply doesn't have it at all.

## Proposed Fix

### 1. Modify `constant/physicalProperties` — Add the missing `species` dictionary

Add a top-level `species` dictionary with proper sub-dict format entries:

```
species
{
    CH4       { value 16.043; };
    O2        { value 31.999; };
    N2        { value 28.014; };
    H2O       { value 18.015; };
    CO2       { value 44.01; };
}
```

Place it at the top level of the file, alongside `thermoType`, `multiComponentMixture`, etc. The `multiComponentMixture` block can remain as-is (it already has `specie` and `multiComponentAssemblies`). The standalone `species` dictionary serves as the source for the mixture to build its internal species map.

### 2. Verify `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` has complete `janaf` data

Ensure all five species (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) have complete `janaf` thermodynamic blocks with `Cp`, `H`, `S`, and `T` coefficients. Missing species will cause errors after this fix resolves the current one.

### Summary

The fundamental issue across 9 attempts is that the `physicalProperties` file was missing a proper `species` dictionary. Previous attempts tried embedding it in `multiComponentMixture` or fixing formats, but the current file simply lacks a top-level `species` entry. Adding the `species` dictionary with `{ value ...; }` sub-dictionary format at the top level of `physicalProperties` should resolve this persistent error.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Add a top-level species dictionary containing entries for CH4 { value 16.043; }, O2 { value 31.999; }, N2 { value 28.014; }, H2O { value 18.015; }, CO2 { value 44.01; } placed before the multiComponentMixture block'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
